reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Real transformers.js inference in a dedicated module Worker (models/florence-2-large/worker.js): dynamic import of the pinned TRANSFORMERS_URL, then Florence2ForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained("onnx-community/Florence-2-large",{dtype:"fp16",device:"webgpu"}) + AutoProcessor. run() does processor.construct_prompts(task) -> processor(image,prompts) -> model.generate({...inputs,do_sample:false,num_beams:1}) -> processor.batch_decode({skip_special_tokens:false}) -> processor.post_process_generation(raw,task,image.size). This is a WebGPU-ONLY demo: worker probeGPU() calls navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() (presence is NOT enough) and createModelLoader is requiresWebGPU:true — on a no-GPU device (and in a no-GPU headless harness) the page shows a LABELLED needs-WebGPU state with enable steps, never a fabricated result. Because the GPU is genuinely unavailable in a no-GPU runner, the real-run output CANNOT be produced there and is NOT faked; the conformance runner records that class as blocked, not pass. Conformance asserts the mechanism: calls-real-inference (source, matches \.generate\(|createModelLoader) and loads-clean (console-clean) are tested-pass; io-shape-semantic is manual-evidenced (awaiting a GPU agent verdict).
rubric.notes: No canned strings in worker.js/florence.js — prompt, raw sequence and parsed result all derive from the real generation. This is the LARGER checkpoint (0.77B vs 0.23B base) — same unified task-prompt API, higher detection/OCR accuracy; worker.js additionally reports the generated token count.

rubric.evidence: Output is task-shaped seq2seq: caption tasks return a string; <OD>/<DENSE_REGION_CAPTION> return {bboxes:[[x1,y1,x2,y2]...],labels}; <OCR_WITH_REGION> returns {quad_boxes,labels}; <REFERRING_EXPRESSION_SEGMENTATION> returns {polygons}. drawOverlay() in florence.js reads parsed[task] and draws boxes/quads/polygons scaled to the canvas; text tasks fill the answer. The 'See inside' panel prints the constructed prompt, the RAW decoded sequence (<loc_###> tokens intact) and the parsed JSON — the genuine pre/post-processing, not a mock.
rubric.notes: One model, many I/O shapes keyed on the exact task token.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/Florence-2-large, linked) plus the original microsoft card, task (image-text-to-text, task-prompted), architecture (DaViT encoder + BART-style seq2seq), params/dtype (0.77B · fp16, needs shader-f16), backend (WebGPU required), download (~1600 MB), license MIT — matching worker.js (fp16/webgpu) exactly. Conformance declares-model-id/task/quantisation are page-text, declares-backend a script assertion.

Loads via shared createModelLoader (modelId:"onnx-community/Florence-2-large", runtime:"transformers.js", dtype:"fp16", sizeMB:1600, requiresWebGPU:true); onReady enables Run. A valid cached current model auto-initialises with no Load click; Download/Re-download/Update + clear-cache surface otherwise. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source) tested-pass; auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on onnx-community/Florence-2-large so the ~1600 MB cache is tracked for the no-silent-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence:
Status is

; the shared loader shows progress + a Retry path. On failure the catch writes status.err ('Task failed: '+message) and resets Run. The needs-WebGPU state is surfaced by the loader (webgpuFallbackHTML: enable steps for chrome://gpu / flags / shader-f16). Conformance live-status-region (dom) + errors-on-the-page (source) tested-pass; honest-progress-retry-offline + webgpu-capability-honest are manual/capability.
rubric.notes: Empty results are honest: renderText shows '(the model returned no text for this task)', renderRegions shows 'No regions were returned…'.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real + keyboard-operable: sample-thumb strip (role=button, tabindex=0, Enter/Space), drop/choose file (accept=image/*), the 8-way task picker (task-btn with aria-pressed), the conditional 'phrase to segment' input (shown only for referring segmentation), and Run (disabled until ready). Selecting a task genuinely steers the model.

No hardcoded output: answer, overlay, prompt, raw sequence and parsed JSON all come from the real generate()+post_process_generation. With no WebGPU the honest needs-WebGPU state shows, never a canned caption/boxes. Conformance no-fake-output is manual-evidenced.

Sample thumbs + the overlay canvas carry alt/aria-label; every button has a text name (conformance buttons-have-names + images-have-alt script assertions tested-pass); status is role=status aria-live; light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system. The canvas overlay is mirrored by a text answer + legend chips + a region count, so results are not canvas-only.
rubric.notes: Minor: box/label text drawn on the canvas is pixels only — the legend + count sentence carry the same info as real text.

rubric.evidence: Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are the tested assertions; vlm-grid is flex-wrap with vlm-img-col/vlm-out-col flex:1 1 300px, canvas max-inline-size:100% height:auto, sample-strip + task-grid flex-wrap — collapses to one column on narrow mobile. parity-both-classes manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Mobile cost (~1600 MB fp16 + WebGPU requirement) surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in the module Worker; the main thread only posts messages and draws a bounded canvas overlay (one pass over parsed regions, not per-pixel), so INP stays low. Conformance inference-off-main-thread (source: new Worker) tested-pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Greedy decode (num_beams:1, do_sample:false) — deterministic, no beam-search cost.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low run heavy work off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread and break up long tasks so the browser can service input and repaint; workers + yielding keep interactions responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Florence2 generate() + processor pre/post-processing run entirely in a dedicated module Worker; the page thread only posts the task/image and draws the parsed overlay.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js new Worker(type:module); conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates, accessible names for controls, loading state
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; semantic HTML + visible focus; mark language for correct shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live for load/run/error, aria-pressed on the task picker, alt on samples, aria-label on the overlay canvas, text legend/answer mirroring the canvas.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two-column layout without horizontal overflow using flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap, intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — vlm-grid flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px columns, canvas max-inline-size:100%, sample/task rows flex-wrap; one column on mobile.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas-rendered content (overlays / generated image) to assistive technology
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name and a text equivalent so it is exposed to assistive tech.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the detection/segmentation overlay canvas has an aria-label and is duplicated by a text answer + legend chips + region count, so the result is not locked inside the canvas.

openQuestions: io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached are manual-evidenced — a GPU-equipped agent run should capture a real caption/boxes screenshot + latency to flip them to pass.
The large checkpoint is ~1.6 GB fp16 — usable on mid-range mobile GPUs, or should phones be steered to the base page?

followUpGoals.goal: On a WebGPU machine, capture retained evidence (caption + object-detection screenshot, raw sequence, latency) to satisfy the io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached manual assertions.
Consider a rendered attention/saliency 'see inside' surface for detection tasks to make grounding legible beyond boxes.

summary: Retrospective code-grounded critique for florence-2-large (onnx-community/Florence-2-large, image-text-to-text, fp16, WebGPU-only, ~1600 MB, MIT). A TASK-PROMPTED vision foundation model: one 0.77B-param network captions, detects, reads (OCR) and segments, steered only by the task token, via Florence2ForConditionalGeneration.generate + construct_prompts + post_process_generation in a dedicated module Worker. Output is task-shaped (text / bboxes+labels / quad_boxes / polygons) drawn on a canvas with a text legend; 'See inside' shows the constructed prompt, RAW decoded sequence and parsed JSON. WebGPU-only with an honest needs-WebGPU fallback — no fabricated output, and in a no-GPU harness the real run is genuinely blocked, not faked. guidanceConsulted: break-up-long-tasks, accessibility, css-layout, expose-canvas-content-to-browser-features. Weakness flagged: io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached remain manual-evidenced pending a GPU-equipped capture.
